info.title: Check missing CT test behavior (@general subagent)

parts.text:
In /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe, investigate the `TestURLFetch_MissingContentTypeNotStripped` test. The concern is that Go's `net/http` server may auto-detect Content-Type even when we set it to empty string. Run this test with verbose output: ```bash cd /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe && go test ./internal/tool/ -run TestURLFetch_MissingContentTypeNotStripped -v -count=1 ``` Also, temporarily add a debug test that checks what Content-Type the server actually sends when we set it to "": ```go func TestURLFetch_DebugContentType(t *testing.T) { ts := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) { w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "") _, _ = w.Write([]byte("

No header

")) })) defer ts.Close() resp, err := http.Get(ts.URL) if err != nil { t.Fatal(err) } defer resp.Body.Close() t.Logf("Content-Type header: %q", resp.Header.Get("Content-Type")) } ``` Add this test, run it, report what Content-Type the server actually sends, then REMOVE the debug test. Return: 1. Does `TestURLFetch_MissingContentTypeNotStripped` currently pass? 2. What Content-Type does the server actually send when we set it to ""?

input.description: Run the specific test with verbose output

state.title: Run the specific test with verbose output

metadata.description: Run the specific test with verbose output

parts.text: Test passes. Now let me read the test file and add the debug test.

state.title: internal/tool/url_fetch_test.go

metadata.preview:
package tool

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"net"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/provider"
)

func TestURLFetch_Success(t *testing.T) {
	ts := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		_, _ = w.Write([]byte("hello world"))
	}))
	defer ts.Close()

parts.text: Got the answer. Now remove the debug test.

Results:

**1. Does `TestURLFetch_MissingContentTypeNotStripped` currently pass?**

Yes — it passes cleanly.

**2. What Content-Type does the server actually send when we set it to `""`?**

`""` — an empty string. Go's `net/http` server does **not** auto-detect or inject a Content-Type when you explicitly call `w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "")` before writing the body.

The concern was valid in principle (Go *does* sniff Content-Type when the header is absent entirely), but `Set("Content-Type", "")` suppresses that sniffing by marking the header as explicitly set. So the test's assumption holds: when the handler calls `Set("Content-Type", "")`, the client receives no Content-Type, and the production code correctly returns the body verbatim without HTML stripping.
